title: Calculating relative frequency
problem: A spinner is spun $50$ times. It lands on blue $15$ times. Find the experimental probability of landing on blue.

steps.instruction: Identify the number of trials and the frequency of the event
steps.working: Total number of spins (trials) $= 50$. Number of times blue appeared (frequency) $= 15$.

title: Comparing experimental and theoretical probability
problem: A fair six-sided die is rolled $60$ times and a $1$ appears $7$ times. Find the experimental probability of rolling a $1$, and compare it to the theoretical probability.

steps.working: For a fair die, each face is equally likely. $P_\text{theo}(1) = \dfrac{1}{6} = \dfrac{10}{60}$.

Experimental: $\dfrac{7}{60} \approx 0.117$. Theoretical: $\dfrac{10}{60} \approx 0.167$. The experimental probability is less than the theoretical probability. This is normal — experimental results vary from trial to trial, and tend to get closer to the theoretical value as the number of trials increases.

answer: $P_\text{exp}(1) = \dfrac{7}{60}$, which is less than the theoretical probability of $\dfrac{1}{6}$.

title: Predicting expected frequency
problem: In an experiment, a bag of counters is sampled with replacement $60$ times and a green counter is drawn $18$ times. If the bag is sampled $300$ times, how many times would you expect to draw green?

steps.instruction: Find the experimental probability of drawing green
Use the experimental probability to find the expected frequency

answer: You would expect to draw green $90$ times.
